_catalog.name: CargoTrack Route Intelligence Catalog
_catalog.disclaimer: This catalog reflects trade regulations as understood at the effective date. Regulations change frequently. Always verify with official regulatory sources before making compliance decisions.

corridors.label: United States → China
corridors.sanctionsNotes: Export control restrictions under Export Administration Regulations (EAR). BIS Entity List, Military End-User List, and Unverified List screening required. Dual-use technology (ECCNs 3A, 3B, 4A, 4E, 5A, 5E) subject to BIS licensing. Huawei and affiliates on Entity List.
requiredDocumentsExport: Certificate of Origin (Form A or CO)
Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
requiredDocumentsImport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
specialRequirements: Section 301 tariff surcharges (List 1-4B) — verify applicable HTSUS subheading
BIS Entity List screening mandatory for all parties
CITES permit required for wildlife/timber
FDA registration mandatory for food, pharma, cosmetics
REACH compliance for chemicals
corridors.tariffNotes: Most Favored Nation (MFN) rates apply + Section 301 additional tariffs 7.5%–25% on most goods
routeRestrictions: Certain semiconductor manufacturing equipment prohibited without BIS license
Advanced AI chips (A100, H100 class) restricted to China
Military and dual-use goods require State Dept / BIS pre-approval

corridors.label: United States → European Union
corridors.sanctionsNotes: Transatlantic allies. No comprehensive sanctions. Individual party screening via OFAC SDN and EU Consolidated List still required.
requiredDocumentsExport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
Certificate of Origin (if preferential tariff claimed)
requiredDocumentsImport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
specialRequirements: EU Entry Summary Declaration (ICS2) required before departure
GDPR compliance for data shipments
REACH registration for chemical imports >1 tonne/year
CE marking mandatory for electronics, machinery, medical devices
Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) for steel, aluminium, cement, fertilizers
corridors.tariffNotes: WTO MFN rates apply. EU Common External Tariff. 5%–6.5% on most manufactured goods.

corridors.label: United States → United Kingdom
corridors.sanctionsNotes: Close allies. OFAC SDN and UK OFSI Consolidated List screening required for all parties.
requiredDocumentsExport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
requiredDocumentsImport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
UK Conformity Assessed (UKCA) marking (post-Brexit regulated products)
specialRequirements: Post-Brexit: No longer EU Single Market — full customs procedures apply
UKCA marking replaces CE for UK Great Britain market
CE marking still accepted until December 2024 for most goods
UK Global Tariff applies (close to EU CET)
Sanitary and Phytosanitary (SPS) checks for food and agricultural products
tradeAgreements: UK-US Trade discussions ongoing (no full FTA as of 2024)
corridors.tariffNotes: UK Global Tariff (UKGT). Generally similar to EU CET.

corridors.label: United States ↔ Mexico
corridors.sanctionsNotes: USMCA partners. Standard OFAC SDN screening. Drug-related cartel designations exist — screen for narco-trafficking related entities.
requiredDocumentsExport: USMCA Certificate of Origin (for preferential tariff)
Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
requiredDocumentsImport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
NOM (Norma Oficial Mexicana) compliance certificate for regulated products
specialRequirements: USMCA Rules of Origin verification critical — confirm regional value content
Maquiladora/IMMEX program considerations for manufacturing imports
Pre-arrival messaging system (PAMS) for cargo security
NOM certification mandatory for electronics, food, cosmetics, textiles
corridors.tariffNotes: USMCA eliminates tariffs on qualifying originating goods. Most goods qualify for 0% under USMCA.
routeRestrictions: Certain agricultural goods subject to TRQs under USMCA

corridors.label: United States ↔ Canada
corridors.sanctionsNotes: USMCA partners. Standard party screening via OFAC and CBSA watch lists.
requiredDocumentsExport: Canada Customs Invoice (CCI) for commercial shipments
specialRequirements: CUSMA/USMCA qualification for duty-free treatment
Food safety: CFIA import requirements for food products
Health Canada requirements for pharma/health products
corridors.tariffNotes: USMCA eliminates tariffs on qualifying originating goods.

corridors.label: European Union → China
corridors.sanctionsNotes: EU-China Comprehensive Agreement on Investment (CAI) suspended (2021). EU dual-use export controls apply. Russian war-related sanctions — scrutinize transshipment via China. BIS Entity List screening for US-content goods.
requiredDocumentsExport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
requiredDocumentsImport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
specialRequirements: EU Dual-Use Regulation (EU) 2021/821 — license required for controlled items
Anti-dumping duties on steel, solar panels, e-bikes from China
CBAM applies to Chinese steel/aluminium/fertilizers/cement
Huawei-related supply chain scrutiny
corridors.tariffNotes: EU Common External Tariff + anti-dumping duties on many Chinese goods.
routeRestrictions: Dual-use goods require EU export license
Russia circumvention risk via China — monitor for re-export potential

corridors.label: United Kingdom ↔ European Union (Post-Brexit)
corridors.sanctionsNotes: Allies. Standard party screening. UK OFSI and EU Consolidated List checks required.
requiredDocumentsExport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
requiredDocumentsImport: EUR.1 or Statement on Origin (for TCA preferential rate)
Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
specialRequirements: UK-EU TCA requires proof of UK/EU originating content for zero-tariff treatment
Rules of Origin: typically 50-55% regional value content required
Northern Ireland Protocol: NI remains in EU single market for goods
SPS border checks: full import controls for food/animal products
UKCA vs CE marking divergence ongoing
corridors.tariffNotes: Zero tariff for qualifying originating goods under TCA. Non-originating goods face EU CET / UK Global Tariff.
routeRestrictions: Full customs formalities — no longer single market
Ireland: complex routing; NI Protocol applies for goods moving NI-EU

corridors.label: Singapore → ASEAN (Intra-ASEAN)
corridors.sanctionsNotes: Intra-ASEAN trade generally clear. Myanmar: targeted sanctions by US/EU/UK on military-related entities. Screen all parties against OFAC SDN.
requiredDocumentsExport: ASEAN Trade in Goods Agreement (ATIGA) Form D (for preferential tariff)
Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
requiredDocumentsImport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
specialRequirements: ATIGA Form D for ASEAN preferential tariff rates (average 0-5%)
RCEP Certificate of Origin for additional preferences
Myanmar: human rights due diligence required — military entity connections
Halal certification for food goods to Malaysia/Indonesia/Brunei
corridors.tariffNotes: ATIGA: most tariff lines at 0% for ASEAN-origin goods. Non-ASEAN goods face MFN rates.

corridors.label: UAE ↔ India
corridors.sanctionsNotes: UAE is a known transshipment hub for sanctions evasion (Iran, Russia). Screen all UAE-based intermediaries carefully. India maintains strategic autonomy and trades with sanctioned countries — verify ultimate consignee/end-use.
requiredDocumentsExport: Certificate of Origin (CEPA Form for preferential rate)
Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
requiredDocumentsImport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
specialRequirements: India-UAE CEPA (2022) — preferential rates for qualifying goods
BIS (Bureau of Indian Standards) mandatory for electronics, toys, steel
FSSAI registration for food imports
Drug Controller General approval for pharma
UAE: Dubai is major re-export hub — verify goods actually originate in UAE for CEPA benefit
corridors.tariffNotes: India applies relatively high MFN tariffs (avg 18%). CEPA provides significant reductions.
routeRestrictions: UAE transshipment risk for Iran/Russia sanctions — enhanced due diligence required

corridors.label: China → India
corridors.sanctionsNotes: Geopolitical tensions post-2020 Galwan. India has banned several Chinese apps/tech. Screen for dual-use and national security concerns.
requiredDocumentsExport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
requiredDocumentsImport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
specialRequirements: India FDI restrictions: Chinese FDI requires government approval (Press Note 3/2020)
Anti-dumping duties on many Chinese goods (steel, solar, chemicals, textiles)
BIS mandatory certification for electronics — often not pre-certified by Chinese manufacturers
Enhanced customs scrutiny for China-origin goods
No bilateral FTA — WTO MFN rates apply (often high for India)
corridors.tariffNotes: India's average applied MFN tariff ~18% + anti-dumping duties on many categories.
routeRestrictions: Technology goods: heightened scrutiny for national security concerns
Some Chinese goods require prior import approval

corridors.label: UAE → European Union
corridors.sanctionsNotes: UAE-based intermediaries frequently used for Russia sanctions evasion. Screen for dual nationality/beneficial ownership. UAE on FATF grey list (periodically). Verify goods truly originate in UAE.
requiredDocumentsExport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
requiredDocumentsImport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
specialRequirements: Russia sanctions: verify goods are not transshipping through UAE to Russia
Anti-money laundering: UAE financial sector scrutiny
REACH compliance for chemicals
CE marking for regulated products
tradeAgreements: EU-GCC FTA under negotiation (not yet in force as of 2024)
corridors.tariffNotes: EU Common External Tariff applies. No preferential rates currently.

corridors.label: United States → Russia
corridors.sanctionsNotes: Comprehensive U.S. sanctions in response to Ukraine invasion. Most goods prohibited. OFAC authorizes very limited exceptions (food, medicine). BIS has restricted virtually all exports. Russia on BIS List. Do NOT process without explicit OFAC/BIS license.
requiredDocumentsExport: BIS Export License (mandatory for virtually all goods)
specialRequirements: COMPREHENSIVE SANCTIONS — HALT for compliance review before any action
EAR99 goods also restricted under Russia/Belarus Sanctions Rule (March 2022)
No U.S. person may facilitate transactions involving Russia without OFAC license
Secondary sanctions risk for facilitators
corridors.tariffNotes: Russia suspended from Normal Trade Relations (NTR) / Most Favored Nation status by U.S.
routeRestrictions: BLOCKED: Virtually all goods require OFAC/BIS license
Secondary sanctions apply to third-country facilitators

corridors.label: United States → Iran
corridors.sanctionsNotes: COMPREHENSIVE OFAC sanctions. Most transactions with Iran are prohibited. Limited exceptions: food, medicine, agricultural commodities (TSRA General License), personal communications. ITSR (Iranian Transactions and Sanctions Regulations) apply.
requiredDocumentsExport: OFAC General License documentation (for exempt categories)
requiredDocumentsImport: Not applicable — importation from Iran to US generally prohibited
specialRequirements: COMPREHENSIVE SANCTIONS — HALT immediately for compliance review
ITSR prohibits most U.S. person transactions with Iran
Third-country banks may refuse USD transactions touching Iran
Secondary sanctions risk: non-U.S. persons assisting Iran sanctions evasion
corridors.tariffNotes: Iran not eligible for U.S. tariff treatment.

corridors.label: United States → North Korea
corridors.sanctionsNotes: COMPREHENSIVE U.S. and UN sanctions. Trading with North Korea is effectively prohibited. UN Security Council has imposed near-total trade embargo. No goods, services, or technology permitted without specific OFAC authorization.
specialRequirements: COMPREHENSIVE SANCTIONS — Do NOT process under any circumstances without OFAC authorization
corridors.tariffNotes: North Korea ineligible for U.S. tariff treatment.
routeRestrictions: BLOCKED: Comprehensive UN and U.S. sanctions

corridors.label: European Union → Russia
corridors.sanctionsNotes: EU has imposed 14 packages of sanctions on Russia since 2022. Comprehensive restrictions on goods, technology, finance, transport. Most goods prohibited. Anti-circumvention rules apply — goods cannot transit through third countries to reach Russia.
requiredDocumentsImport: Most Russian goods banned from EU import
specialRequirements: COMPREHENSIVE SANCTIONS — HALT for compliance review
EU anti-circumvention rules: cannot use UAE, Turkey, Armenia, Georgia as transit
500+ listed individuals and entities fully frozen
corridors.tariffNotes: Russia removed from EU MFN status.

corridors.label: United Kingdom → India
corridors.sanctionsNotes: No comprehensive sanctions. Standard party screening via OFAC SDN and UK OFSI.
requiredDocumentsExport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
requiredDocumentsImport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
specialRequirements: UK-India FTA under negotiation (not concluded as of 2024)
India applies GSP to developing country origin goods — UK no longer qualifies as developing
BIS mandatory for electronics, steel products
FSSAI for food imports
corridors.tariffNotes: India MFN rates apply (avg 18%). No preferential rate currently.

corridors.label: Singapore → China
corridors.sanctionsNotes: China export controls apply. BIS Entity List screening for goods with US content. Singapore is a key re-export hub — verify actual origin of goods for US-content rules.
requiredDocumentsExport: Certificate of Origin (Form E for ASEAN-China FTA)
Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
Export Permit (for controlled goods — Singapore Customs)
specialRequirements: ASEAN-China FTA Form E for preferential rates
Singapore Strategic Goods Control Act — re-export of controlled goods requires permit
Semiconductor equipment: heightened scrutiny due to US export control extraterritorial reach
Verify no US-origin content restrictions apply
corridors.tariffNotes: ACFTA Form E provides preferential rates. Most ASEAN-origin goods qualify for reduced China tariffs.

corridors.label: Japan ↔ United States
corridors.sanctionsNotes: Close allies. Standard party screening via OFAC SDN. Japan maintains independent export controls (FEFTA) aligned with allied frameworks.
requiredDocumentsExport: Certificate of Origin (for JUSTA preference claims)
Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
requiredDocumentsImport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
specialRequirements: Japan-U.S. Trade Agreement (2020) — limited coverage, not comprehensive FTA
Section 232 tariffs: steel 25%, aluminium 10% (some exemptions for Japan)
Japan semiconductor export controls aligned with US BIS rules
corridors.tariffNotes: MFN rates generally apply. Japan-US agreement covers select agricultural and industrial goods.

corridors.label: Korea ↔ United States
corridors.sanctionsNotes: Close allies. Standard party screening via OFAC SDN. KORUS FTA in force.
requiredDocumentsExport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
requiredDocumentsImport: KORUS Certificate of Origin (for duty reduction)
Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
specialRequirements: KORUS FTA origin requirements: regional value content verification
ITA obligations: no tariffs on tech/semiconductors
Automotive: tariff phase-out schedule applies
corridors.tariffNotes: KORUS eliminates most tariffs. Key sectors: electronics, machinery at 0%.

corridors.label: Australia → China
corridors.sanctionsNotes: Australia-China relations strained (2020-2023 trade disputes largely resolved). China imposed unofficial bans on Australian coal, wine, barley, beef — most lifted by 2023. BIS Entity List screening for US-content goods. Ongoing geopolitical caution.
requiredDocumentsExport: ChAFTA Certificate of Origin (Form for China-Australia FTA)
Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
requiredDocumentsImport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
specialRequirements: China-Australia FTA (ChAFTA) — preferential rates, tariff elimination schedule
Agricultural trade: monitor for informal restrictions
FIRB approval for Chinese investment in Australian assets
Critical minerals: heightened political scrutiny
corridors.tariffNotes: ChAFTA provides significant tariff reductions. Most agricultural and resource goods at 0%.

corridors.label: India → European Union
corridors.sanctionsNotes: No comprehensive sanctions. Standard party screening. India-Russia trade: EU may scrutinize Indian re-exports with Russian content.
requiredDocumentsExport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
requiredDocumentsImport: GSP Form A (for preference) / REX Statement
Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
specialRequirements: EU GSP (Generalized System of Preferences) — India qualifies for preferential rates
India-EU FTA under negotiation (not concluded 2024)
REACH compliance for chemicals
CE marking for regulated products
Anti-dumping: EU AD duties on some Indian steel, textiles
corridors.tariffNotes: India benefits from EU GSP preferential rates. EU CET applies to non-GSP goods.

corridors.label: Domestic (Same Country)
corridors.sanctionsNotes: Standard domestic party screening applies. OFAC SDN list screening required for all transactions.
specialRequirements: No customs clearance required
DG goods require carrier compliance with domestic DG regulations
HAZMAT placarding for road transport (if DG)
corridors.tariffNotes: No import duties.

corridors.label: International (Generic)
corridors.sanctionsNotes: Unknown corridor. Standard party screening via OFAC SDN, EU Consolidated List, and UN Security Council list required. Additional screening recommended.
requiredDocumentsExport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
requiredDocumentsImport: Bill of Lading or Air Waybill
specialRequirements: Verify applicable bilateral or regional trade agreements
Consult destination country customs authority for specific requirements
Screen all parties against OFAC, UN, and EU sanctions lists
corridors.tariffNotes: Destination country MFN tariffs apply unless FTA qualifies goods for preference.
